model_narrative: An airline must decide how to deploy its fleet across a set of routes when passenger demand on each route is uncertain and described by several possible demand states. For every aircraft type and route we choose how many aircraft of that type to fly on that route, which in turn sets how much seating capacity each route receives. When the realized demand on a route exceeds the capacity placed there, the excess passengers are turned away, and turning passengers away costs lost revenue. The airline wants to minimize the total operating cost of the assignment plus the expected cost of passengers turned away across all demand states.

sets.name: i
sets.doc: the aircraft types in the fleet

sets.name: j
sets.doc: the routes that aircraft can be assigned to serve

sets.name: h
sets.doc: the possible demand states on a route, indexing the discrete scenarios of how many passengers want to travel

sets.name: ij
sets.doc: the set of feasible (aircraft type, route) pairs; only these combinations may be operated, and any aircraft-route combination outside this set is not allowed

sets.name: jh
sets.doc: the set of valid (route, demand state) pairs that can occur

params.name: dd
params.doc: the number of passengers demanding travel on a route in a given demand state, in passengers

params.name: lambda_
params.doc: the probability that a route experiences a given demand state; for each route these probabilities sum to one

params.name: c
params.doc: the operating cost of assigning one aircraft of a type to a route, in thousands of dollars

params.name: p
params.doc: the passenger seating capacity that one aircraft of a type provides when flown on a route, in passengers per aircraft

params.name: aa
params.doc: the number of aircraft of each type available in the fleet, in aircraft

params.name: k
params.doc: the revenue lost per unit of passengers turned away on a route, in thousands of dollars per hundred bumped passengers

vars.name: x
vars.doc: the number of aircraft of each type assigned to each route

vars.name: z
vars.doc: the total passenger seating capacity allocated to each route by the assigned aircraft, in passengers

vars.name: bh
vars.doc: the number of passengers turned away on a route in a given demand state, in passengers

vars.name: phi
vars.doc: the total expected cost, in thousands of dollars

vars.name: oc
vars.doc: the total operating cost of the aircraft assignment, in thousands of dollars

description: The fleet is limited, so we cannot deploy more aircraft of a type than we actually own. For each aircraft type, the total number of that type's aircraft sent out across all the routes it is allowed to serve must not exceed how many of that type are available.

For every aircraft type and route we choose how many aircraft of that type to fly on that route, which in turn sets how much seating capacity each route receives. description: Each route ends up with a certain amount of seating capacity that comes from the aircraft assigned to it. For every route, the seating capacity allocated to that route equals the total seats provided by all the aircraft assigned to it, where each assigned aircraft contributes the seats it offers on that route. Only allowed aircraft and route combinations contribute.

When the realized demand on a route exceeds the capacity placed there, the excess passengers are turned away, and turning passengers away costs lost revenue. model_narrative: An airline must decide how to deploy its fleet across a set of routes when passenger demand on each route is uncertain and described by several possible demand states. For every aircraft type and route we choose how many aircraft of that type to fly on that route, which in turn sets how much seating capacity each route receives. When the realized demand on a route exceeds the capacity placed there, the excess passengers are turned away, and turning passengers away costs lost revenue. The airline wants to minimize the total operating cost of the assignment plus the expected cost of passengers turned away across all demand states.

sets.doc: the set of feasible (aircraft type, route) pairs; only these combinations may be operated, and any aircraft-route combination outside this set is not allowed

vars.name: phi
vars.doc: the total expected cost, in thousands of dollars

vars.name: oc
vars.doc: the total operating cost of the aircraft assignment, in thousands of dollars

description: To build the complete model, enforce the following relationships in order. First, the fleet is limited, so we cannot deploy more aircraft of a type than we actually own. For each aircraft type, the total number of that type's aircraft sent out across all the routes it is allowed to serve must not exceed how many of that type are available. Second, each route ends up with a certain amount of seating capacity that comes from the aircraft assigned to it. For every route, the seating capacity allocated to that route equals the total seats provided by all the aircraft assigned to it, where each assigned aircraft contributes the seats it offers on that route. Only allowed aircraft and route combinations contribute. Third, on each route, the capacity placed there has to cover everyone who wants to travel except for those we deliberately turn away. For every route and every demand state, the number of passengers demanding travel, after subtracting the passengers turned away, must not exceed the seating capacity allocated to that route. Finally, the total operating cost reflects what it costs to fly the chosen assignment. Set the operating cost equal to the sum over every allowed aircraft and route combination of the per aircraft operating cost times the number of those aircraft assigned to that route.
